description: AiSpec(spec card) emitting task.completed at the kernel write entry is ALLOWED (intentionally left so per PR7 decision (b))
note: Characterization surprise: the in-tx role gate has no rule denying task.completed to AiSpec — sections 2/2.5 don't list it and section 3 only inspects AiCodex/AiClaude actors. The 'spec must not report task completion' rule lives ONLY in the MCP soft gate (mcp_server/tools/emit.rs task_complete -> require_role(Worker)). PR7 decision (b): INTENTIONALLY left allow — recorder-gate ENFORCE (PR7b-ii) already restricts wave-state recording to the wave root, so this kernel-gate AiSpec allow is a low-risk defense-in-depth backstop behind the soft gate. Flipping it to deny (which forces re-authoring accepted verdicts as ActorId::Kernel + a dispatcher self-push fix) is tracked in #733.

description: AiCodex(report card) emitting task.completed into ANOTHER card's scope is DENIED (closed in PR7b-ii)
note: Was a characterization surprise: the ReportCard arm (role_gate.rs section 3) performed NO self-scope cross-check, unlike the Worker arm. Closed in PR7b-ii — the ReportCard arm now runs enforce_card_self_scope, so a report-card-bound actor can no longer write outside its own card scope.

description: AiCodex(report card) emitting a Wave-scoped task.completed is DENIED (closed in PR7b-ii)
note: Same ReportCard self-scope tightening as the previous vector, one scope level up (closed in PR7b-ii).

description: AiCodex(report card) is still refused wave.updated — the wave-authority branch fires before the ReportCard arm
AiCodex(report card) is still refused codex.worker_requested — the dispatch-authority branch fires before the ReportCard arm
